definition: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient ratchets input bytes toward an exact literal constant (FOURCC, magic bytes, enum code, keyword token, single byte) via operand-distance bucketing; any input closer to the constant earns a new bucket and is retained as corpus, enabling step-wise hill-climbing that naive/cmplog edge-only feedback cannot provide.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a file-format magic that fails the ELF/MachO/WASM probes yet matches a later (PE) probe, reaching the 1556 true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose leading magic bytes pass all three earlier format probes as non-matches (not ELF, not MachO, not WebAssembly's `\0asm`) so object_file is still null at line 1556, while being close enough to a recognizable format (PE/COFF) that TryOpenPEFile is exercised. Concretely: bytes at offsets 0x00-0x03 NOT equal to the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d, instead drifting toward other format magics (e.g. 4c 46 / 7f 45 4c 4c 46 / 01 01 ...).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-WASM leading bytes (seed 3 starts 4c 46 'LF', seed 4 starts 7f 45 4c 4c 46, seed 1 starts 01 01) and the per-role overlay marks lines 1557-1558 (TryOpenPEFile) as [W] winner-only and shows the blocker at L1556 taking T=60 F=0 for the winner, i.e. the winner repeatedly reaches and takes the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser-blocking seed begins 00 61 73 6d (the WebAssembly '\0asm' magic), so object_file becomes non-null at line 1552 (TryOpenWebAssemblyFile) and line 1556's true branch is never taken (winner W L1556 = T=60 F=0 vs loser L L1556 = T=0 F=120). The byte-diff confirms losers are pinned to a-s-m at offsets 0x01-0x03.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP feedback: the integer/string magic-byte comparisons inside each TryOpen*File probe emit a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket, so inputs that get progressively closer to (or away from) a given format magic are preserved as new corpus. This gradient lets value_profile steer leading bytes away from the WASM-magic basin that traps naive and toward a downstream-format magic, exposing the 1556 true branch. naive has only edge-hit feedback and cannot sense partial magic-byte proximity, so it stays stuck on whatever magic its corpus already matched.
